_comment: Perf baseline for interpretthis. Numbers are criterion's reported median / mean / std-dev for each bench (nanoseconds per iteration), captured at base_sha against an empty Tools registry. Future slices run `cargo bench` and assert median is within the per-workload envelope vs the median below: eval <=1.2x, workloads <=1.15x, containers <=1.1x. The criterion_id matches the criterion bench id printed in the cargo-bench output and identifies the corresponding target/criterion/<id>/ directory.

_capture.tools_registry: empty (interpreter loop only, no host-tool dispatch)
_capture._rename_note: 2026-06-07: bench files reorganised by interpreter layer; groups renamed (arithmetic->eval, comprehension_10k->containers, pipeline_realistic->workloads). Workloads are byte-identical; numbers were NOT re-captured. Old target/criterion/<old-group>/ directories are stale and can be deleted on developer machines.

eval.rationale: Pure interpreter dispatch is the hottest path; Track A dispatch refactor must stay within 1.2x or the gate trips.

workloads.rationale: Realistic LLM-extraction shape mined from code_interpreter integration tests; tighter envelope reflects production-cost sensitivity.

containers.rationale: Comprehensions are iteration-protocol dominant; Track A4 must preserve container-build cost within 1.1x.
